claims.claim_text: The module's Knight Capital 2012 cross-reference points to a real August 1, 2012 trading incident caused by failed deployment and control safeguards.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters, opening sentence
claims.rationale: The SEC press release directly documents the August 1, 2012 Knight Capital incident and the control failures behind it, but sec.gov is off the current allowlist.

claims.claim_text: DVC is a common way to keep data and model version information in Git while storing large artifacts in external storage for reproducible ML work.
claims.span_hint: section: 2. Version The Whole ML System, second paragraph
claims.rationale: The DVC README explicitly says DVC keeps version info in Git, stores data and models in cloud storage, and supports reproducible ML projects.

claims.claim_text: Running `dvc add` creates or updates a lightweight `.dvc` tracking file and adds the target to `.gitignore` so the payload does not get committed to Git directly.
claims.span_hint: section: 2. Version The Whole ML System, paragraph beginning 'DVC demonstrates the pointer-file model clearly'
claims.rationale: The DVC `add` command reference directly describes `.dvc` file creation and `.gitignore` updates, but dvc.org is off the current allowlist.

claims.claim_text: DVC pipelines declare dependencies and outputs, and DVC reruns only the stages impacted by relevant changes.
claims.span_hint: section: 2. Version The Whole ML System, paragraph beginning 'DVC pipelines add another important idea'
claims.rationale: The DVC README states that pipelines specify dependencies and outputs and that DVC runs only the steps impacted by changes.

claims.claim_text: Testing an ML system goes beyond ordinary software testing and adds data validation, trained model quality evaluation, and model validation.
claims.span_hint: section: 3. Test The Failure Mode You Actually Fear, opening paragraph
claims.rationale: Google's MLOps architecture guide explicitly says ML testing is more involved than ordinary software testing and names these additional validation layers.

claims.claim_text: In real-world ML systems, only a small fraction of the overall system is the ML code and the surrounding infrastructure is much larger.
claims.rationale: Google's MLOps guide explicitly states that only a small fraction of a real-world ML system is ML code and that the surrounding elements are vast and complex.

claims.claim_text: An ML model can degrade without a code deployment when the production data profile moves away from the training distribution.
claims.rationale: Google's MLOps guide says ML models can degrade due to evolving data profiles, not only because of coding defects.

claims.claim_text: A Kubernetes Deployment is meant to maintain desired state for long-running workloads and replacement Pods are created when a Pod in the Deployment fails.
claims.span_hint: section: 6. Run ML Workloads With The Right Kubernetes Primitive, second paragraph
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes self-healing documentation explicitly says Deployments replace failed Pods to maintain the specified number of replicas.

claims.claim_text: A Kubernetes Job is the run-to-completion primitive for one-off tasks and tracks completion and retries for those tasks.
claims.span_hint: section: 6. Run ML Workloads With The Right Kubernetes Primitive, paragraph beginning 'A Kubernetes `Job` matches that run-to-completion model'
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes Jobs documentation explicitly defines Jobs as one-off tasks that run to completion and describes their retry and completion tracking behavior.

further_reading.title: MLOps: Continuous delivery and automation pipelines in machine learning
further_reading.why_relevant: This is the strongest high-level reference for the module's framing of reproducibility, testing, and operating ML systems in production.

further_reading.title: DVC: Data Versioning and ML Experiments
further_reading.why_relevant: It directly explains the Git-plus-artifact-storage model, pipelines, and experiment workflow used throughout the DVC sections.

further_reading.title: Kubernetes Jobs
further_reading.why_relevant: It directly supports the module's guidance on using Jobs rather than Deployments for finite ML validation and training workloads.

notes: No shared citation pool exists for this section, so `section_pool_ref` is null. Excluded Mermaid diagrams, code fences, quiz items, hands-on exercise steps, and the Common Mistakes table per pipeline rules. The main off-allowlist gaps are sec.gov for Knight Capital and dvc.org for the exact `dvc add` command semantics.
